title: India and the Rebalancing of Asia
gist: IISS landing page for C. Raja Mohan's Adelphi book arguing China's rise has forced India to abandon its traditional ambivalence about Chinese power and become an active strategic balancer, primarily by deepening partnership with the US — while preserving autonomy amid an unpredictable second Trump administration. Promotional page that nonetheless states several concrete strategic theses.

insights.claim: C. Raja Mohan argues China's rise has compelled India to discard its traditional ambivalence about Chinese power and actively counter Beijing by building national power and partnerships, primarily with the US.
insights.speaker: C. Raja Mohan
insights.so_what: Codifies the structural India-US convergence against China — supportive of US-India defence/tech deals and of India's strategic-autonomy hedging across supply chains.
insights.quote: China’s rise has compelled India to discard its traditional ambivalence about Chinese power and counter Beijing by strengthening its own national power and developing partnerships with other states, primarily the US
insights.locator: In this Adelphi book, C. Raja Mohan delineates

insights.claim: The book identifies India as the regional state whose contradictions with an assertive Beijing are the sharpest, making US balancing against China especially significant for New Delhi.
insights.speaker: C. Raja Mohan
insights.so_what: Frames the India-China relationship as the region's most combustible bilateral — a key watch-item for border-flashpoint and Indo-Pacific escalation risk.
insights.quote: the contradictions between New Delhi and an increasingly assertive Beijing are the sharpest in the region
insights.locator: The United States’ interest in balancing

insights.claim: IISS states China today outranks its major proximate competitors India and Japan on economic and defence indices 'by huge margins,' framing a structurally asymmetric Asian balance.
insights.speaker: C. Raja Mohan
insights.so_what: The size of China's lead sets the timescale for any multipolar rebalancing — relevant to long-horizon Asian defence-spend and alliance-formation trends.
insights.quote: today outranks its major proximate competitors – India and Japan – on economic and defence indices by huge margins
insights.locator: In recent decades, China has risen

insights.claim: The analysis flags the second Trump administration's envisioned policy changes as a key uncertainty for the emerging US-India strategic relationship.
insights.speaker: C. Raja Mohan
insights.so_what: US policy unpredictability is the main swing risk to the US-India alignment thesis — a variable to monitor for defence and trade deal continuity.
insights.quote: potential challenges to the emerging US–India strategic relationship

insights.claim: The book positions India as seeking to shape a multipolar Asia while preserving strategic autonomy rather than fully aligning with the US.
insights.speaker: C. Raja Mohan
insights.so_what: India's autonomy doctrine caps how far the US-India bloc can be relied on — relevant to defence-procurement diversification (e.g. continued Russian and indigenous sourcing).
insights.quote: how India seeks to shape a multipolar Asia while preserving autonomy in a complex strategic environment
